evals.prompt: Help me learn about fractions. I'm an adult refreshing my high school math. I want exercises I can practice with.
evals.expected_output: A diataxis.toml structure document and at least one content file per quadrant covering basic fractions, plus at least one marimo exercise notebook.

evals.prompt: Create diataxis documentation for this Python project
evals.expected_output: A diataxis.toml structure document and content files documenting the skill-diataxis project (its build pipeline, structure schema, scoring rubric, etc.)

evals.prompt: The tutorial on basic operations is too abstract. Use more concrete examples with real numbers. Also add a section about mixed numbers.
evals.expected_output: Updated diataxis.toml with guidance reflecting the feedback, revised tutorial with concrete numeric examples, and a new or expanded section on mixed numbers.

evals.prompt: I want to learn how to fine tune an LLM on my local computer. Teach me how.
evals.expected_output: A diataxis.toml structure covering LLM fine-tuning topics (dataset prep, model selection, training config, evaluation), content across all four quadrants, and interactive marimo exercise notebooks walking through each step.

evals.prompt: Score the diataxis documentation in evals/fixtures/flabby-tutorial/diataxis/
evals.expected_output: A scores.toml with prose_quality scores. The tutorial (getting-started.md) should score 3 or below on prose_quality due to pervasive throat-clearing, passive voice, filler words, and redundant phrasing. The scorer should cite specific examples of flabby prose.
expectations: prose_quality score for tutorials/getting-started.md is 3 or lower
Justification for tutorials/getting-started.md cites at least 3 specific filler phrases from the text
Justification mentions passive voice as an issue in tutorials/getting-started.md
prose_quality score for reference/common-vars.md is 4 or higher
prose_quality score for explanation/why-env-vars.md is 4 or higher
A scores.toml file is created with a prose_quality field for every scored file

evals.prompt: Score the diataxis documentation in evals/fixtures/subtle-howto/diataxis/
evals.expected_output: A scores.toml with prose_quality scores. The how-to (pre-commit-linting.md) should score 3 on prose_quality — it reads OK at first glance but is riddled with passive voice and vague claims. The scorer should not be fooled by surface-level readability.
expectations: prose_quality score for howto/pre-commit-linting.md is 3 or lower
Justification identifies passive voice as an issue in howto/pre-commit-linting.md
Justification identifies at least one vague claim (e.g. 'This tool is helpful', 'This configuration is useful')
prose_quality score for tutorials/first-hook.md is 4 or higher
prose_quality score for explanation/why-hooks.md is 4 or higher
A scores.toml file is created with a prose_quality field for every scored file

evals.prompt: Score the diataxis documentation in evals/fixtures/tight-reference/diataxis/
evals.expected_output: A scores.toml with prose_quality scores. All files should score 4 or higher on prose_quality — the prose is tight, active, and concrete throughout. The scorer should not penalize clean writing.
expectations: prose_quality score for reference/codes.md is 4 or higher
prose_quality score for tutorials/first-api.md is 4 or higher
prose_quality score for explanation/rest-semantics.md is 4 or higher
prose_quality score for howto/error-handling.md is 4 or higher
No prose_quality score in the entire project is below 4
A scores.toml file is created with a prose_quality field for every scored file

evals.prompt: This diataxis/ directory was created with an older version of the skill, before we migrated to Hugo. Upgrade it to the current format so I can build it with Hugo.
evals.expected_output: Every quadrant markdown file gets TOML frontmatter, hugo.toml/Makefile/go.mod are scaffolded, each quadrant has an _index.md landing page with canonical section weights, body H1s are removed, .html links become pretty-URL directory links, and the retired-tool guidance references (pandoc, mmdc, uv run diataxis build) are surfaced to the user.
expectations: hugo.toml exists in the upgraded diataxis/ directory
Makefile exists in the upgraded diataxis/ directory
go.mod exists in the upgraded diataxis/ directory
Every quadrant markdown file (tutorials/, howto/, reference/, explanation/) begins with a +++ TOML frontmatter block
No quadrant markdown file contains a body H1 (# Title at the start of the body after the frontmatter)
Each of the four quadrant directories contains an _index.md with title, weight, and a bulleted link list
_index.md section weights are explanation=10, tutorials=20, howto=30, reference=40
index.md has frontmatter with [cascade] type = "docs"
No markdown link target in authored files ends with .html or /index.html
The agent runs nu checks/run-checks.nu against the upgraded directory and reports that all structural checks pass
The agent identifies diataxis.toml guidance fields that still reference pandoc, mmdc, or 'uv run diataxis build' and flags them for review

evals.prompt: Score the diataxis documentation in evals/fixtures/pre-hugo-project/diataxis/
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that the fixture is in pre-Hugo format, upgrades it (or clearly explains that the upgrade must run first), and then produces a scores.toml with prose_quality scores. The skill must not attempt to score against the current Hugo rubric without first upgrading.
expectations: The agent detects the pre-Hugo state before scoring (either by inspecting for missing hugo.toml/frontmatter or by running the upgrade --check pass)
The agent either runs upgrade_to_hugo.py or explicitly surfaces the pre-Hugo state to the user before attempting to score
If the agent proceeds to score, a scores.toml is produced with prose_quality fields for every scored file
If the agent proceeds to score, the upgraded directory passes nu checks/run-checks.nu before qualitative scoring begins

evals.prompt: Check whether the diataxis documentation in evals/fixtures/tight-reference/diataxis/ is in the current Hugo format or an older format.
evals.expected_output: The skill reports that the fixture lacks Hugo frontmatter, _index.md landing pages, and hugo.toml, and describes it as pre-Hugo (or equivalent). The skill does not perform the upgrade unless asked — this prompt is just a detection request.
expectations: The agent identifies the fixture as pre-Hugo (or equivalent phrasing: 'old format', 'needs upgrading', 'missing Hugo frontmatter')
The agent cites specific missing artifacts (frontmatter, _index.md, hugo.toml, or similar)
The agent does not modify any file in the fixture
The agent offers to run the upgrade or explains how the user can run it

evals.prompt: This diataxis project was authored before we migrated to Hugo. Please bring it fully up to date — both the file format AND the guidance in diataxis.toml. Several guidance fields still reference the retired tools (pandoc, mmdc) and old build commands (uv run diataxis build). Update the structure document so the guidance reflects the current Hugo + make workflow, then regenerate any content that's now out of sync.
evals.expected_output: Two-stage work: (1) the mechanical upgrade (frontmatter, _index.md, scaffolding); (2) integrated revision of diataxis.toml guidance fields that referenced retired tools, without appending a 'REVISION FEEDBACK:' block — the updated guidance should read as if written from scratch with current knowledge. Affected content files should be regenerated to match the new guidance.
expectations: The mechanical upgrade ran (frontmatter present, _index.md files created, hugo.toml scaffolded)
The guidance field for topics/getting-started/tutorials no longer mentions pandoc
The guidance field for topics/getting-started/howto no longer mentions pandoc
The guidance field for topics/build-pipeline/howto no longer mentions mmdc
The guidance field for topics/build-pipeline/howto references 'make build' or 'hugo' instead of 'uv run diataxis build'
No guidance field contains a labeled 'REVISION FEEDBACK:' block (the feedback must be integrated, not appended)
The covers lists no longer reference pandoc, mmdc, or 'uv run diataxis' commands (or the agent explicitly justified leaving them)
Affected content files (tutorials/first-project.md, howto/install-and-setup.md, howto/build-and-serve.md, reference/cli-reference.md) were regenerated and no longer mention the retired tools
